ety: inf. n. iv of فضل 'to excel'
def: s.m. Causing to excel or to increase; benefiting.
adj. 1. Deranged; disarranged; tangled. 2. Deteriorated; spoiled; injured. Jo kuchh igar digar ho jāēgī, to jawāb kaun degā? 3. Out of joint; unhinged; topsy-turvy.

ety: S. एकादशन्
def: adj. Eleven.
adj. Eleventh.
adj. Eleventh.

ety: دگر redupl.
def: adj. Disordered, disarranged, deranged, topsy-turvy, tangled, confused; spoiled, injured.
adj. Eleven ( = igārah, q.v.). adj. Eleven.
more: adj. (dakh.) Eleven: see اگاره igārah, and گياره gyārah.

def: (inf. iv. of غمز) s. m. Detraction: but, sometimes used for اغماض q. v.

ety: inf. n. iv of غلق 'to shut, close,' &c.
def: s.m. Abstruseness; obstacle; — adj. Abstruse.
more: (inf. iv. of غلق) s. m. 1. The being abstruse or difficult to be understood, abstruseness. 2. An obstacle.

ety: inf. n. iv of غلم 'to be lustful'
def: s.m. Inflaming with desire, provoking to venery; — sodomy.
more: (inf. iv. of غلم) s. m. 1. Inflaming with desire, provoking to venery. 2. Sodomy.

def: s.m. A sodomite (syn. launḍe-bāz).

ety: inf. n. iv of غمض 'to connive' (at)
def: s.m. Connivance, neglect; dissimulation; winking (at), ogling, coquetry; superciliousness
more: (inf. iv. of غمض) s. m. Dissimulation, connivance, superciliousness, ogling, coquetry. اغماض کرنا ig̠ẖmāẓ karnā, To wink or connive at.
subs: (-kā), To wink (at), connive (at), to overlook, shut the eyes (to), to pass unnoticed or unheeded, to pass over or by; to refrain from, to omit.

def: adj. & s.m. Supercilious, fastidious; proud, arrogant; — one who is supercilious, &c., a coxcomb. (N.B. The use of this word appears to be confined to Soutnern India.)
more: adj. Contemptuous, supercilious, fastidious, insolent, arrogant, haughty, proud, scornful; a coxcomb ( Harris ).

ety: inf. n. iv of غرق 'to sink'
def: s.m. Causing to sink or drown, drowning, submersion; (in rhetoric) exaggerated praise or censure.
more: (inf. iv. of غرق) s. m. Causing to drown, submersion.

ety: inf. n. viii of غنم 'to carry off as plunder'
def: s.m. Taking or seizing as plunder or as a prize; seizing, carrying off.
more: (inf. viii. of غنم) Taking or seizing as plunder or as a prize, seizing.

ety: inf. n. iv of غوي 'to err'
def: s.m. Leading astray; seduction; solicitation, temptation, instigation: ig̠wā karnā, v.t. To lead astray, seduce, tempt, &c.
more: (inf. iv. of غوي Heb. Erred, acted perversely) s. m. Seduction, temptation, instigation. اغوا کرنا ig̠ẖwā karnā, To seduce, lead astray, tempt.
ex: ارے میری بیٹی اغوا ہو گئی!
are merī beṭī iġvā ho gaī!
Oh no, my daughter has been kidnapped!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اغوا (iġwā), from Arabic إِغْوَاء (ʔiḡwāʔ), from أَغْوَى (ʔaḡwā, “to lure, to tempt”).

def: here, in this place or world
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit इह (ihá), doublet of یَہاں (yahā̃).

def: pron. This, &c. = yah, q.v. pron. (prox. demons.) This; this person; — he; she; it; — adv. In this, herein; here; — to the extent or degree (that), to this degree
subs: Here he is come, here he comes
See here, here it is (cf. wah, and wah dekho, &c.)
This is the condition; on condition (that), provided
conj. Neither this nor that; neither
(emphat.) This very one, the very same

def: s.f. Desire; attempt; exertion, energy.
adj. This = yěh, q.v.
subs: (iha+amutra), adv. Here and there; in this world and the next.

def: adv. Here, &c. = yahāṅ, q.v.

ety: for A. اهانة, inf. iv of هون 'to be light,' &c.
def: s.f. Contempt, disdain; affront, insult, indignity, slight; slander, calumny
more: (inf. iv. of هون) s. f. Contempt, disdain, affront, insult, indignity, enmity. اهانت کرنا ihānat karnā, To contemn.

def: to enclose
(inf. iv. of حدت Heb. He renewed) s. m. Act of making or producing any thing new, production, invention, innovation.
pron. emphat. This very, &c. = ihi, ihiṅ, yahī, q.v.
more: interj. O! sign of the vocative.
pron. This very: see اه ihi.

def: adj. This very = ihīṅ, yahīṅ, q.q.v.
pron. emphat. = ihī, yahī, q.q.v.
contingency
to do a favour, oblige
consciousness, emotion, feeling, perception, realisation
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِحْسَاس (ihsās), from Arabic إِحْسَاس (ʔiḥsās, “feeling”).

def: sensational
wety: Borrowed from Persian احساساتی (ehsâsâti).

ety: inf. n. iv of وجب 'to be necessary,' &c.
def: s.m. Rendering necessary; (in Logic) affirmation (as opp. to 'privation'); assent; (in Moḥammadan law) the first proposal made by one of the parties in negotiating or concluding a bargain
more: (inf. iv. of وجوب) s. m. 1. Rendering necessary. 2. (in logick) Affirmation (opposite to privation). 3. (in Muhammadan law) The first proposal made by one of the parties in negociating or concluding a bargain.
subs: s.m. Proposal and assent or acceptance (in a negotiation of marriage, &c.).

def: n. m. Proposal and acceptance (in a negociation of marriage). The offer and assent must be declared before witnesses to render the marriage valid.

ety: inf. n. iv of جوب
def: s.f. Answering, replying to ( = jawāb denā); granting a favourable reply; accepting (a petition or prayer); acceptance, approval; compliance, consent, sanction, assent; — action of the bowels, motion, evacuation, stool ( = dast).
more: n. f. Granting a favorable reply; answering (a petition or prayer); consent; admission; acknowledgment; acceptance; assent; approbation; compliance; acquiescence. 2. Sanction; grant; concession. 3. Evacuation; motion; stool (dast) . Khul-kar ijābat nahiṅ hotī .
(inf. iv. of جوب) s. f. Answering (of prayer or of a petition), consent, approbation, compliance.

ety: inf. n. iv of وجد 'to be found'; 'to exist'
def: s.m. Creation, production; invention, contrivance
more: (inf. iv. of وجود) 1. s. m. Invention, contrivance. 2. adj. Invented. ايجادکرنا ījād karnā, To invent, contrive, devise.
subs: v.t. To create, produce, originate, invent, design, discover

def: s.f. = P. ازار izār, q.v.: — ijār-band = izār-band.
more: n. m. H. उ up, जड़ root. S. जिरि to destroy. 1. Demolition; destruction; devastation; ravage; havoc; ruin; waste; desolation. 2. An uninhabited place; a desert; wilderness; a ruin; a desolate country. 1. Naṅgā khaṛā ujāṛ meṅ, "Hai koī kapṛe le?" Prov. Stark naked, in the wild, he cries out "dare any body strip me?" 2. Bā'rah-dūnī ujāṛ' . A boundless desert. ujāṛ sū'rat , E. bhoṅḍī —W. adj. Ugly; ill-featured; unsightly; deformed; bad looking. (Applied to a woman who appears without jewels or ornaments.) ujāṛ mūṅh , E. n. m. An ugly, or unlucky face; an ill-omened countenance. Sir jhāṛ, mūṅh ujāṛ (yā pahāṛ) . Prov. A head of hair like a hedge, a face like an ogre. ujāṛ', ū'jaṛ , adj. Demolished; dilapidated; depopulated; laid waste; spoiled; ruined; deserted; uncultivated; abandoned; desolated.

def: monopolistic

ety: from اجر 'to recompense'
def: s.m. Letting land on lease, letting a house, &c. on rent or hire; land taken on lease; a house, &c. taken on rent or hire; the giving of usufructs for a compensation; a privilege or an income of a variable amount sold for a fixed sum; lease, farm, contract, rent, hire; right, title, monopoly (arising from a lease, contract, &c.)
more: n. m. آجَرَ compensated. 1. A privilege, or income, sold or let for a fixed sum. 2. Lease; lands taken on a farm or lease. Yeh gāoṅ kis ke ijārah meṅ hai? 3. Farm; contract; monopoly; exclusive right or authority. 1. Kuchh āp kā ijārah nahīṅ . 2. Toṛan āë chārā, aur khet pe ijārā . Prov. Came to forage, claimed the field. 4. A farm or lease of the revenue of a village or district, also of customs, or collection of any description, as of customary fees or allowances.
s. m. 1. Hire, rent, a privilege or income of variable amount sold or let for a fixed sum. 2. A farm.
subs: s.m. Deed of lease, lease; agreement; title-deed
ijār-dār, s.m. Lease-holder, lessee; farmer, tenant; contractor; farmer of land or revenue; one who has purchased the labour of another; holder of a monopoly; proprietor
v.t. To farm, lease, let, give out on contract
v.n. To bind oneself, be responsible (for); to engage; agree
v.t. To take on lease, or hire, or contract, &c., to farm
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِجَارَة (ʔijāra).

def: monopoly

n. m. 1. A farmer; lessee; tenant; a farmer of land or revenue: one who has purchased the labour of another; the holder of a monopoly. 2. Sole master, or proprietor. Aise tum hī ho kyā Brij ke ijār-dār? Song. Holī. ijā'rah de'nā , v. a. To farm; lease; let; give a contract; let in farm (ṭhekā denā). ijārā kar'nā , v. n. To take upon oneself; to be responsible for; to engage; to bind oneself. Jārā lage, to iskā kartā hūṅ maiṅ ijāra, Jiskā kalejah, yāro, sardī ne hove mārā, Nau dām ke woh mujh se le jāë til ke laḍḍū . Nazīr. If cold you feel, then do I make a bond with you, Whoseever liver, friends, is struck with cold, be't you, For half a farthing you may have a til laḍḍā . (The poet has woven his verses out of a familiar street cry).
more: s. m. A farmer of land or of revenue: one who has purchased the labour of another: the holder of a monopoly.

def: n. m. A lease; a document under which a lease or farm is held.

ety: inf. n. iv of وجز 'to be short, &c.
def: s.m. Abridging, curtailing, epitomizing (a book, &c.); abridgment.
more: (inf. iv. of وجز) s. m. Abridgment, the abridging of a discourse or book.

ety: inf. n. iv of جوز 'To go or pass through or along'
def: s.f. Permission, liberty, leave, authority, sanction; leave to depart, dismissal; authority or liberty to do anything; licence; passport
more: n. f. A. جاز for جوز Passed, or made current, (money), held to be right. 1. Sanction; a carte blanche . 2. Permission; liberty; leave. 3. Dismissal; leave to depart. Kyoṅ Sāhab, ijāzat hai? 4. A license; permit; pass; passport. ijā'zat chāh'nā , v. n. To ask leave or permission to depart.
(inf. iv. of جوز) s. f. Permission, leave, dispensation, sanction, commands, orders. اجازت خواه ijāzat-ḵẖẉāh, adj. Asking permission. اجازت دینا ijāzat denā, To permit.
subs: s.m. One who seeks permission or sanction (for any purpose); applicant; petitioner
v.t. To grant leave or permission, to permit, allow, let; to give (one) leave to depart; to grant, admit, concede, comply with; to authorize, sanction; to license; to empower
s.m. A written document by which a permission is conferred; authority; licence.
ex: وہ تمہیں اس کام کرنے کی اجازت نہیں دیں گے۔
vo tumhẽ is kām karne kī ijāzat nahī̃ dẽ ge.
They will not permit you to do such a thing.
(With your permission) let us be on our way
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِجَازَه (ijāza), borrowed from Arabic إِجَازَة (ʔijāza).

def: v. a. 1. To permit; allow; let; grant. 2. To give leave, or permission. 3. To give leave to depart. 4. To admit; concede; tolerate; suffer; accord; comply with. 5. To empower; warrant; authorize.
imprimatur
Unid. n. m. A petitioner; suppliant; applicant.
direct plural of اِجازَت (ijāzat, “permission”)
License to sell. ijāzat milnā , v. n. To obtain permission or leave.

n. m. A written order or permission; a deed of authorization (Mah.); the anumatī patra of Hindu law, or the written permission of the husband to his wife to adopt a son after his decease. — i-tabnīyāt (Mah.) An authority to adopt a son. — wā'pas le'nā , v. n. To withdraw a license.
oblique plural of اِجازَت (ijāzat, “permission”)
n. m. amboh , Cor. of. A. ازدحام Multitude; crowd ( bhīṛ ).

ety: inf. n. iv of جل 'to be great'
def: s.m. Magnifying, exalting, honouring; magnificence, glory, honour.
more: (inf. iv. of جلّ) s. m. The bestowing or acknowledging of greatness; magnificence, glory, honour, respect.

ety: inf. n. iv of جلس 'to sit'
def: s.f. The act of sitting of a court of justice; sitting, sessions; a court
more: n. f. جَلَسَ he sat. The act of sitting of a court of justice; sitting; sessions; a court. ijlās karnā . v. n. To sit (in court); preside. bar-sar-i-ijlās, ijlās meṅ , adv. In court; in the presence of; while sitting in court.
(inf. iv. of جلس) s. f. Seating, causing to sit.
subs: v.n. To sit in court; to preside
In the court (of, -ke); in the presence (of), before.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اجلاس (ijlās).

ety: inf. n. iv of جمع 'To assemble, collect'
def: s.m. Act of assembling; assembly; council; senate; court of justice; crowd; collection; amount; whole; — concurrence, agreement
more: (inf. iv. of جمع) s. m. Act of assembling, an assembly, senate, council, court of justice, a crowd, collection, amount, whole. اجماع امت ijmāﻌ-i-ummat, s. m. General assembly of the people, unanimous consent.
subs: s.m. General assembly or convocation of the people; unanimous consent
s.m. The unanimous authority of the companions of Moḥammad on a point of law.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic إِجْمَاع (ʔijmāʕ).

ety: inf. n. iv of جمل 'To collect'
def: s.m. Summing up; recapitulation; abstract, abridgment, summary, compendium, epitome, synopsis; — joint occupancy or possession.
more: n. m. Ped. جمل many strands of a rope put together, aggregate. 1. Sum and substance; recapitulation; resumè . 2. An abstract; summary; compendium; precis; abridgement; epitome; digest; synopsis. 3. Joint occupancy or possession.
(inf. iv. of جمل) s. m. An abstract, compendious account, synopsis.

def: adj. Gross; abridged; succinct; compendious; brief; undivided, held in common (as an estate).
more: adj. Ped. Gross; compendious; abridged; succinct.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اجمالی (ijmālī), from the Arabic verb أَجْمَلَ (ʔajmala, “to summarize”).

ety: inf. n. iv of جري 'to pass along quickly, to flow'
def: s.m. Causing to circulate, putting in circulation, giving currency to, making current; issuing; putting into force or execution; carrying into effect; issue; execution; performance, practice
more: adj. See. ujār . Dark; gloomy; cheerless, 1. Dulhan ke āne se, merā ujṛā ghar bas gayā . Wom. 2. Ujṛe ghar kā balaiṅḍā . Prov. The ridge pole of a deserted house. (contemptuously applied to a very tall, thin, and awkward person).
(inf. iv. of جري) s. m. Performance. اجرا کرنا ijrā karnā, To perform.
s. m. Causing to flow or to circulate; circulation.
subs: v.t. To issue, put in force, perform, execute, &c.

def: n. m. Ped, جري passed. 1. Giving currency or circulation; putting in execution. 2. Issue; execution; service; enforcement; carrying into effect; performance.

ety: inf. n. viii of جہد 'to strive, labour'
def: s.m. Exerting the faculties to the utmost; exertion, effort, earnest endeavour; diligence; — waging war against infidels.
more: (inf. viii. of جهد) s. m. Exertion, effort.

ety: inf. n. viii of جمع
def: s.m. Act of collecting, assembling or gathering together; agreeing together (in opinion); combining, conspiring, leaguing together (against, -par); assemblage, gathering, collection, congregation, accumulation, afflux; combination, league; (in astron.) conjunction
more: (inf. viii. of جمع) s. m. 1. Act of assembling or gathering together; agreeing together (in opinion), assemblage, collection, accumulation, afflux. 2. Conjunction (in astronomy).
subs: To assemble, &c.; to combine or conspire (against, -par)
adv. Collectively, in a body; unanimously
(-par), To be combined, or in league (against); a conjunction (of stars or planets) to bear (upon), or be adverse (to).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِجْتِمَاع (ijtimāʕ).

ety: inf. n. viii of جنب 'to put aside or away'
def: s.m. Keeping away or aloof (from, -se), turning aside or away (from), avoiding, shunning; refraining (from); abstinence; continence; self-control, self-denial; temperance; moderation; austerity; self-mortification
more: n. m. Ped. جنب Put away. 1. Keeping aloof; steering clear of (a person); turning aside from; avoiding. Āp kyoṅ ham se is qaḍar ijtināb rakhte haiṅ . 2. Self-control; continence; self-denial; abnegation. 3. Abstinence; fasting. 4. Temperance; moderation; sobriety. 5. Austerity; moderation; ascetìcìsm.
(inf. viii. of جنب) s. m. Turning aside from, avoiding; abstinence, continence.
wety: From Arabic اِجْتِنَاب (ijtināb), verbal noun of اِجْتَنَبَ (ijtanaba).

def: evasive
to confect

ety: contr. from ايك ek, and used in compn.
def: adj. One
more: adj. Used in Comp. for ek one.
subs: s.m. Either of the dishes of a pair of scales
ik-peṅćā, s.m. An ornament for the head
ik-ṭak, adj. Fixed, staring (look or eyes)
adv. In one place, together, united
adj. & adv. Of one mind, unanimous, unwavering, without wavering
(or rājya), s.m. Universal sway or rule
ik-sāṅ, adj. Even, level, plane; equal; similar, same; resembling ( = yak-sāṅ)
v.t. To make even, level, smooth, plane; to dig and plough

ety: inf. n. of عاقب 'to punish,' &c.; iii of عقب 'to follow after'
def: s.m. Punishment, chastisement; torture.
more: (from عقب Heb. ) s. m. f. An eagle. ¶ iﻌḳāb, s. m. Chastisement.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُقَاب (uqāb), borrowed from Arabic عُقَاب (ʕuqāb).

def: s.f. = ايکادشی ekā-daśī, q.v. s.f. The eleventh day of a lunar fortnight.
more: n. f. S. एकादशी . The eleventh day of the lunar fortnight, on which the Hindūs generally keep fast.

ety: S. एक+धारक, or वार+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Single, of one piece; simple (uncompounded, not complex); univalved (in Bot.)
subs: s.f. Simple Rule of Three (syn. trai-rāsik; arbaʻ mutanāsiba).

def: adj. 1. Single; of one piece; the opposite of complex or compound. 2. ( Bot .). One-valved; simple; having no subordinate parts or distinct ramifications. akahrā badan , adj. Thin; lean; lank (body). akahrā bol ; A. mufrad lafz , n. f. ( Gram .). A simple word, in contradistinction to a compound word. ikahrī lāg ; A. arbā mutnāsibā ; S. traī-rās'ika . The simple rule of three.
s.f. A unit; the unit's place in numeration
subs: The figure in the unit's place, the unit.
wety: From ایک (ek, “one”) + ـَائی (-āī).

def: n. f. A unit.

ety: inf. n. iv of قوم 'to be at a stand'
def: s.f. Resting, staying; abode, residence, dwelling.
more: (inf. iv. of قیامت) s. f. Resting, staying, abode, residence, dwelling.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِقَامَت (iqāmat), from Arabic إِقَامَة (ʔiqāma).

def: = اکائی ikāʼī, q.v. s.f. A unit; the unit's place in numeration
subs: The figure in the unit's place, the unit.

def: adj. Ninety-one.

ety: S. एकनवतिः
def: adj. Ninety-one.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit एकनवति (ekanavati)

def: s.m. The letter इ i

ety: S. एकाशीति
def: adj. Eighty-one.

ety: corr. from the English
def: s.f. An act, enactment, statute.

adv. = اکهٿا ikhaṭṭā, q.v. v.t. To collect, get or bring together, gather together, assemble; to amass, accumulate, store, stock, heap or pile up, stack, group; to call together, convoke, convene; to sum up, add up, total; to concentrate, consolidate
more: ( एक One, and स्थान Place) adj. Collected together, in one place.
subs: v.t. To collect, amass, store up, accumulate, treasure up, &c.
or ho-jānā, v.n. To come together, gather, collect, assemble, congregate, flock, herd, crowd; to be added or summed up
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀏𑀕𑀢𑁆𑀣 (egattha), from Sanskrit ए॒क॒स्थ (ekasthá), from ए॒क (eká, “one”) + स्थ (stha, “standing”)..

ety: S. एकत्रिंशत्
def: adj. Thirty-one.
more: adj. S. एकत्रिंशत् Thirty-one. ik-chhat rāj kar'nā , v. a. To hold universal dominion.

ety: S. एक+वर्ण+क
def: adj. (prov.), Of one kind, unmixed, pure, picked.
more: n. m. H. ek nā not one. Picked; unmixed; pure. Ikaunā gehūṅ .

def: s.m. An eruption on the leg.
more: E n. m. An eruption in the leg.
W. n. m. The cross bar on which the pulley turns.

ety: S. एकपंचाशत्
def: adj. Fifty-one.

ety: inf. n. iv of قبل 'to receive favourably,' &c.
def: s.m. Prosperity, good fortune, auspices, felicity; prestige; — acceptance (of a bond, bill, &c.); admission of a claim, &c.), confession, acknowledgment; acquiescence, assent
more: (inf. iv. of قبل) s. m. Prosperity, good fortune, auspices. اقباله ikbāluhu, His good fortune, &c. اقبالہم iḳbāluhum, Their good fortune, &c.
subs: Confession of judgment; admission of a claim
adj. Prosperous, of good fortune, fortunate, happy, felicitous, lucky, auspicious
His good fortune, &c.; iqbālu-hum, Their good fortune, &c.

def: adj. 1. Prosperous; happy; fortunate ( bhāgwān ); felicitous. 2. Lucky; propitious ( subh ). iqbāl-mandī , n. f. Prosperity; good fortune.
adv. v.s.v. اك ik.
To hold universal dominion.
adj. v.s.v. اك ik.

ety: inf. n. iv of قدم 'to go before or forward'
def: s.m. Sending on before, causing to go before; pushing forward; boldness, intrepidity, audacity, resolution, firmness, spirit; endeavour, effort, diligence, attention
more: (inf. iv. of قدم) s. m. Intrepidity, endeavour, effort, diligence, spirit, resolution, firmness, attention.
subs: (-par), To enter boldly (upon), go boldly forward (in), resolutely undertake; to advance (against).

def: One moment.

ety: S. इक्षु
def: s.f. (& m. in Hindī), Sugar-cane ( = ūkh, q.v.)
n. f. Sugarcane. See اُوکهـ.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀇𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀼 (ikkhu), from Sanskrit इक्षु (ikṣu).

ety: S. एक+स्थितः, rt. स्था
def: v.t. To collect, get or bring together, gather together, assemble; to amass, accumulate, store, stock, heap or pile up, stack, group; to call together, convoke, convene; to sum up, add up, total; to concentrate, consolidate
subs: v.t. To collect, amass, store up, accumulate, treasure up, &c.

Material cause. illat o mālūl ; S. kāran kārya , Cause and effect ( sabab o musabbab ). ba-illat , adv. Through; for; in a charge of ( ba-sabab ). jurm kī illat meṅ , For any offence. harf-i-illat . The interchangeable letters وا and ي in Arabic.

(from علّ Heb. To attempt, to cause) s. f. 1. Disease, defect. 2. Cause, pretence, pretext. 3. Filth, dirt. (Plur. علل iﻌlal ). حرف علت ḥarf-i-iﻌllat, The interchangeable letter و,ا or ي. Every material substance is said to have four causes: علت صوري iﻌllat-i-ṣūrī, Formal cause, i. e. that form in which its essence consists: علت غاٴي iﻌllat-i-g̠ẖāʼī, Final cause, or purpose for which it was made. علت فاعلي iﻌllat-i-fāʼiﻌlī, Efficient cause (as the maker, if the work of man): علت مادي iﻌllat-i-māddī, Material cause, or the matter of which the thing is made.

Kachchī kalī kachnār kī, aur toṛat man pachhtāë, Ham ḍareṅ ijlām se, kahīṅ kamar na ṭūṭ jā/?/ë . 2nd speaker. Ām phale ladā phadī, ki ḍālī jhuk jhuk jāë, Tum chaṛho sauk se, ki hai Rām sahāë . Pop. Jogiṛā . 2. In law . Accusation; charge; indictment; impeachment; arraignment; bill of indictment. 3. Reproach; discredit; disgrace; calumny. ilzām dhar'nā , v. a. To impute a fault; find fault with. ilzām de'nā, ilzām lagā'nā , v. a. 1. To impute; taunt, or brand with; denounce. Phr. To lay to one's door; to lay to one's charge; to throw in one's teeth. 2. To accuse; criminate; arraign; indict; to fix a charge; inform against; charge; impeach.
(inf. iv. of لزم) s. m. Conviction, confutation, reproof, censure, blame, proof. الزام پانا ilzām pānā, To be convinced, confuted, &c.
subs: (-par), to impute a fault (to), find fault (with), accuse (of)
v.t. To impute; to denounce; to blame, accuse, indict, charge (with), inform against
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian الزام (ilzām, “requirement, commitment, tie”), from Arabic إِلْزَام (ʔilzām).

ety: S. इमि
def: adv. Thus, so, to such a degree ( = aisā: used chiefly in poetry).

ety: old P. ima; Zend aem; S. idam, ayam
def: adj. This ( = īṅ
subs: The form im occurs only in comp. e.g.)
im-roz, adv. This day, to-day; — imroz-fardā, To-day or to-morrow, soon; procrastination ( = āj-kal, q.v.); īmroz-fardā karnā, To put off; to procrastinate; to shilly-shally

ety: inf. n. iv. of ومٲ 'to make a sign'
def: s.f. Sign, nod, beck, hint, suggestion, indirect reference or allusion; emblem, symptom.
more: (inf. iv. of ومأ) s. f. Sign, nod, wink, hint, beck, emblem, symptom.

ety: v.n. fr. عمد 'to stay, prop,' &c.
def: s.m. A stay, prop, support; confidence, reliance, trust; — a pillar, column ( = ʻamūd)
more: (Heb. from عمد Heb. To support, to stand) s. m. 1. A lofty pillar. 2. Confidence, reliance, trust, support.
subs: 'Pillar of the state'; a proper name.

ety: for A. امالة, inf. n. iv of ميل 'to incline'
def: s.m. Causing to incline, bending; (in Gram.) giving to fatḥa a sound like that of kasra (as when kitāb is pronounced as if written kitīb).

s.m. One who is followed or imitated; exemplar, guide, leader, head, head of a religion (and especially of the Mohammadan religion), patriarch; priest; minister or reader of a mosque; — a large bead in a rosary (which remains fixed in the hand, and is not turned over in counting); — the flag or standard borne in procession before the taʻziya on the first ten days of Moḥarram
more: n. m. Patriarch; prelate; chief; spiritual head (of the Mahomedans).

s. m. 1. A leader in religious affairs, a prelate, patriarch, priest, chief. 2. A large bead in a rosary, which remains fixed in the hand and is not turned over in counting (called by the Hindūs sumeru ).
subs: s.m. (dim. imām-bāṛī, s.f.), The place to which the taʻziya is conveyed and kept in the Moḥarram, and where offerings are made to the dead; a building in which the festival of the Moḥarram is celebrated, and in which services are held in commemoration of the death of ʻAlī and his sons Hasan and Husain; (the same building is sometimes used as a mausoleum for the family of the founder)
s.m. The protecting Imām, one's guardian saint
or paisā, s.m. A piece of money dedicated to Imām zāmin, and fastened on the arm of a person about to leave home, as a protection from evil spirits and the difficulties of the way.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمَام (imām), borrowed from Arabic إِمَام (ʔimām).

def: a shrine dedicated to Imam Hussain
wety: From اِمام (imām, “Imam”) + بَارْگاہ (bārgāh, “mosque”).

def: The protecting imām or imām zāmin kā rupayā, yā paisā . A piece of coin dedicated to Imām Zāmin and fastened on the arm of a person about to leave home, as a protection from evil spirits and the difficulties of the way.

ety: for A. عمامة, v.n. fr. عمّ 'to comprehend,' &c.
def: s.m. The sheet or cloth that one winds upon the head, a turban; a tiara, a crown.
more: n. m. A sheet wrapped round the head as a turban; a tiara.
s. m. A turband, a tiara.

ety: for A. امامة
def: s.f. The office of an Imām.
more: s. f. Office of امام imām.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمَامَت (imāmat), borrowed from Arabic إِمَامة (ʔimāma), from إِمَام (ʔimām) + ـَة (-a).

def: n. m. 1. The place where Mahomedans deposit the tāziah , and where offerings are made to the dead. 2. A building in which the festival of the Moharrum is celebrated, and service held in commemoration of the deaths of ٴAli and his sons Hasan and Husain. Sometimes it is used as a masoleum for the family of the founder.
s. m. The place where the Muhammadans celebrate the Muharram.
s. f. The place where the Muhammadans celebrate the Muharram.

ety: corr. of P. hāwan+dasta
def: s.m. Pestle and mortar.
Illit. n. m. Cor. of P. hāvan-dasta . Pestle and mortar.
adj. Of or relating to an imām.
more: adj. Relating to an imām. imām'yā , n. m. An epithet assumed by the followers of Alī ; a Mahomedan sect; Shiahs, who make Alī the rightful imām immediately after Mohammad.

ety: for A. امامية
def: adj. = imāmī, q.v.: — s.m. An epithet assumed by the followers of 'Alī; the sect of Shīʻas (who hold that 'Alī is the lawful and true imām in immediate succession to Mohammad).
more: (Followers) of the Imām; name of a Muhammadan sect, who make Alī the rightful imām immediately after Muhammad.

ety: inf. n. iv of معن 'to go fast,' &c.
def: s.m. Sharpening, rendering acute or penetrating; acuteness, penetration
subs: s.m. Penetrating glance, attentive or close regard or look.

ety: inf. n. iv of امن 'To be safe or secure'
def: s.m. Belief (particularly in God, and in His word and apostles, &c.); faith, religion, creed; conscience; good faith, trustworthiness, integrity
more: (inf. iv. of امن Heb. Trusted, was secure) s. m. Faith, belief, religion, conscience, credit. ايمان دار īmān-dār, Faithful, conscientious, honest, trusty, believer. ايمان داري īmān-dār-ī, s. f. Faithfulness, conscientiousness, honesty, fidelity, trustiness.
subs: To sell one's conscience; to break one's word, faith, &c.; to sacrifice one's integrity, honour, &c.; to prove faithless or dishonest
adj. Faithful, true, conscientious; — s.m. A believer
adj. (f. -ī), Honest, true, fair, conscientious, trustworthy, trusty
To sacrifice or lose honour, integrity, &c. = īmān bećnā, q.v.
(-par), To believe (in), become a convert (to); to have or put faith (in), repose trust or confidence (in), rely (on); to credit, give credit (to)
īmān-meṅ farq ānā, To have one's honesty or probity shaken or disturbed, to prove faithless, dishonest, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِیمَان (īmān), borrowed from Arabic إِيمَان (ʔīmān), from آمَنَ (ʔāmana). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi ایمان (ayman).

def: s.m. Name of a metre in Hindī versification; name of a musical mode.
corr. for ايمان īmān, q.v.
adj. P. دار keep. Opp. of be-īmān . q. v. Conscientious; honest; true; upright; just; trustworthy; faithful.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ایماندار (imān-dār), from Arabic إيمان (ʔīmān).

def: n. f. Truth; honesty ( īmān , 2).
adj. Of the (Musalman) faith; faithful.
oblique plural of اِیمَان (īmān, “faith; belief”)
the Emirates, short for the United Arab Emirates
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِمَارَات (ʔimārāt).

ety: for A. عمارة, inf. n. of عمر 'to stay, abide,' &c.
def: s.f. Building; habitation and cultivation; that by which a place is rendered habitable; a building, structure, edifice; a habitation; a fortification.
more: n. f. عمر lived, inhabited. Any large building; an edifice. Imārat gārat . Prov. House building is ruination. imārat-i-sarkārī , n. f. Public works or buildings. imāratī gaz , n. m. A mason's yard. mahakma-i-imārat-i-sarkārī , G. G. n. m. Public works department.
(from عمر Heb. ) s. f. 1. That by which a place becomes habitable, a publick edifice, a building, structure, cultivation, building. 2. A habitation. 3. A fortification. (Plur. عمارات).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عمارت ('imārat), from Arabic عِمَارَة (ʕimāra).

ety: for A. امارة, v.n. of امر 'to command'
def: s.f. Possession of command; the office, and authority of a commander, governor, &c. (amīr); district under the authority of a governor; a government; command, authority, power, dominion, rule, sovereignty; dignity, grandeur, state, magnificence, pomp, display; increase, abundance, prosperity.
more: s. f. Government, lordship, office or dignity of امیر amīr.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمَارَت (imārat), borrowed from Arabic إِمَارَة (ʔimāra), from أَمَرَ (ʔamara).

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻimārat, q.v.), Buildings, edifices, &c.
builder

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻimārat
def: adj. Of, or relating to, building, &c.
subs: s.m. A mason's yard, or rule.

def: s.m. = S ڐمبها डिम्भा ḍimbhā, s.f. New-born child; young of an animal; infant, child; — blockhead, idiot, fool.

ety: inf. n. vii of بسط 'to spread, expand'
def: s.f. Gladness, joy, delight, cheerfulness, recreation, mirth.
more: (inf. vii. of بسط) s. f. Gladness, joy, delight, cheerfulness, recreation, mirth.

ety: inf. n. iv of مدّ 'to help'
def: s.m. Assisting, aiding, abetting; help, succour ( = madad); donation, gift, endowment.
more: (inf. iv. of مدّ) s. f. Assisting, aiding, succour, help.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِمْدَاد (ʔimdād).

ety: inf. n. iv of مکن 'to hold rank,' &c.
def: s.m. Possibility, practicability; power; contingent existence (in contradistinction to wujūb or necessary existence).
more: (inf. iv. of مکن) s. m. Possibility; contingent existence (in opposition to وجوب Necessity, or necessary existence).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمْکَان (imkān), borrowed from Arabic إِمْكَان (ʔimkān), from أَمْكَنَ (ʔamkana). First attested in 1708 as Middle Hindi امکان (amkan /⁠imkān⁠/).

def: adj. Possible, potential; contingent.

ety: inf. n. iv of ملأ 'to fill'
def: s.m. Filling up, completing; inditing, writing correctly; orthography, dictation
more: (inf. iv. of ملأ) s. m. Filling up, completing; orthography, writing correctly.
subs: imlā-navīs, s.m. One who writes correctly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمْلَا (imlā), from Arabic إِمْلَاء (ʔimlāʔ).

ety: for A. عملة ʻimlat
def: s.m. Operation, produce of work; a mode or manner of work.
wety: From Arabic عِمْلَة (ʕimla).

ety: inf. n. iv of ملك 'to possess'
def: s.m. lit. 'Putting in possession'; possessions, &c. = amlāk, q.v.
more: s. A freehold property which has reverted to the zamīndār for want of an owner ( Trans. of the Agricultural, &c. Society of India, vol. i. p. 177).
